Welcome to Smart Question Bank
docs.text: Welcome! This is the Official Documentation Site for the Smart Question Bank project. Here you will find everything related to the system architecture, setup guides, API endpoints, and development standards.

docs.title: 🎯 Project Objectives & Overview
docs.text: The Smart Question Bank is designed to automate, secure, and streamline the process of question generation, storage, and examination management.

docs.title: Key Goals:
docs.text:
• Efficiency: Reduce manual effort in creating balanced question papers.
  • Security: Ensure zero leaks and secure access control for core modules.
  • Scalability: Built on a robust tech stack capable of handling heavy concurrent user traffic.

docs.title: How to Navigate this Docs Site:
docs.text:
Use the Left Sidebar to navigate through the requirements, API references, developer setup instructions, and deployment pipelines.

docs.title: CI/CD Deployment Pipeline
docs.text: The Smart Question Bank project utilizes GitHub Actions to automate automated testing, linting, and continuous delivery to staging/production environments.

docs.title: 1. Workflow Architecture
docs.text: Every time a developer interacts with the GitHub repository, the pipeline runs automated checks based on the target branch:

docs.title: 2. Continuous Integration (CI) Checks
docs.text:
On every Pull Request (PR) submitted against the main branch, the following automated workflow triggers instantly:

  1. Environment Setup: Spins up a fresh Ubuntu container running Node.js v20.
  2. Dependency Installation: Runs npm ci to cleanly install required packages.
  3. Linting Verification: Executes npm run lint to check for coding standard violations.
  4. Test Execution: Runs npm run test to verify no existing features are broken.

🛑 Hard Rule: A Pull Request cannot be merged into the main branch if any of the CI pipeline stages fail.

docs.title: 3. GitHub Actions Configuration (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
docs.text:
For your reference, here is the automated configuration script running in our backend:

```yaml name: Node.js CI

on: push: branches: [ "main" ] pull_request: branches: [ "main" ]

jobs: build_and_test: runs-on: ubuntu-latest

steps:
- name: Checkout repository
  uses: actions/checkout@v4

- name: Setup Node.js
  uses: actions/setup-node@v4
  with:
    node-version: '20.x'
    cache: 'npm'

- name: Install dependencies
  run: npm ci

- name: Run Linter
  run: npm run lint --if-present

- name: Execute Unit Tests
  run: npm test

docs.title: Coding & Docstring Standards
docs.text: To maintain code quality, readability, and consistency across the Smart Question Bank project, all developers must adhere to the following coding standards.

docs.title: 1. Naming Conventions
docs.text:
We follow standard JavaScript/TypeScript naming conventions:

  • Variables & Functions: camelCase (e.g., generateQuestion(), isValidUser)
  • Classes & React Components: PascalCase (e.g., QuestionPaper, SidebarComponent)
  • Database Tables & Columns: snake_case (e.g., question_banks, user_id)
  • Constants: UPPER_CASE (e.g., MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS, JWT_SECRET)

docs.title: 2. Code Comments & Docstrings Guide
docs.text: Do not just write what the code does; explain why it does it if the logic is complex. We use standard JSDoc formatting for all functions.

docs.title: Example for Backend API Controller:
docs.text: ```javascript /* * Generates a randomized question paper based on difficulty rules. * @param {string} courseId - The unique identifier of the course. * @param {Object} dynamicRules - The difficulty distribution (e.g., { easy: 0.4, hard: 0.6 }). * @returns {Promise

docs.title: Environment URLs
docs.text:
• Staging: https://staging.myproject.com
  • Production: https://myproject.com

docs.title: Rollback Procedures
docs.text: If a production release introduces a critical error, run the following workflow in GitHub Actions: 1. Navigate to Actions -> Production Deploy. 2. Select Run Workflow and choose the previous stable release tag (e.g., v1.2.4). 3. Click Run.

docs.title: Branch Naming Conventions
docs.text:
• feature/ for new features (e.g., feature/login-page)
  • bugfix/ for bug fixes (e.g., bugfix/fix-jwt-expiry)
  • hotfix/ for critical production hotfixes

docs.title: Pull Request (PR) Workflow
docs.text:
1. Pull the latest code from main.
  2. Create your feature branch.
  3. Commit using conventional messages (e.g., feat: add password reset button).
  4. Push to origin and open a PR against main.
  5. Require at least 1 senior developer approval before merging.

docs.title: Glossary & Terms
docs.text:
Definitions of project-specific terms and business logic acronyms:

  • SKU: Stock Keeping Unit (Unique identifier for products).
  • Gateway ID: The unique transaction token generated by the payment provider.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Ensure you have the following installed locally: * Node.js >= 20.0.0 * Docker Desktop

docs.title: Setup Instructions
docs.text: <ol> <li>Clone the repository:    ```bash    git clone     cd project-name

docs.title: Live Monitoring Dashboard
docs.text:
• Application metrics can be tracked via our AWS CloudWatch dashboard.
  • Error exceptions and crash logs are tracked inside Sentry.

docs.title: Secret Management
docs.text:
• Never commit secrets: Any .env, private keys, or API tokens pushed to the repository will trigger an automatic security alert and require immediate key rotation.
  • Use GitHub Secrets for production environment injections.

docs.title: Compliance
docs.text:
• Passwords must be hashed using bcrypt with a salt round of 10.
  • Session tokens (JWT) must expire within 15 minutes.

docs.title: Functional Requirements
docs.text:
1. Users must be able to register using OAuth (Google/GitHub).
  2. Admins can view the system analytics dashboard.

docs.title: Non-Functional Requirements
docs.text:
• Security: All passwords must be hashed using bcrypt before saving.
  • Performance: API response time must be under 300ms.

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
The project uses the following core technologies:

Layer Technology Version Frontend React / Next.js v14.x Backend Node.js / Express v20.x Database PostgreSQL v15.x DevOps Docker / GitHub Actions -

docs.title: Issue: Port 3000 already in use
docs.text:
• Symptom: Server fails to start with EADDRINUSE.
  • Fix: Run kill -9 $(lsof -t -i:3000) (Mac/Linux) or stop the process via Windows Task Manager.
